channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Citadel Season 2 Review: Stronger Plot, Character Depth, and Spy Action
item.description: The second season of Prime Video's spy series 'Citadel' shows a marked improvement over its first outing, addressing much of the indifference it faced in 2023. Across seven episodes, the writing is mo...

item.title: EU Prepares Contingency Plans Amid Potential Summer Jet Fuel Shortages
item.description: As the war wey dey between US, Israel, and Iran don close the Strait of Hormuz, Europe dey face wahala for jet fuel supply. Normally, about 20% of the kerosene wey European airlines dey use dey pass t...

item.title: Mwananchi Journalists Recognised in East Africa Merck Media Awards 2025
item.description: Journalists from Mwananchi Communications Limited have made Tanzania proud by winning top positions in the 2025 Merck Foundation Media Recognition Awards. Mr Abeid Othman, editor of Mwananchi newspape...

item.title: Katie Hind Reveals Pressure Tactics by Celebrity PRs to Control Media Coverage
item.description: Katie Hind, a veteran showbusiness journalist, highlights the increasingly aggressive tactics used by celebrity PR teams to control media narratives and shield clients from scrutiny. Hind details inci...

item.title: Woman secretly filmed on smart glasses and asked for payment to remove video
item.description: Alice, not her real name, experienced distress when she was covertly filmed by a man wearing smart glasses while walking into a London shopping centre. Unaware she was being recorded, she later discov...

item.title: Government Approves Promotions for Over 219,000 Public Servants to Boost Efficiency
item.description: Dar es Salaam — The Tanzanian government has officially approved promotions for 219,042 public servants for the 2025/2026 financial year, aiming to boost staff morale and improve efficiency in public ...

item.title: Tesla China-made EV sales increase 36 percent year-over-year in April
item.description: According to data from the China Passenger Car Association, Tesla’s electric vehicles produced at its Shanghai factory saw a significant 36 percent year-on-year increase in sales for April 2026. This ...

item.title: Platform Petroleum Opens Modern ICT Centre in Obonoma to Empower Niger Delta Youths
item.description: Platform Petroleum Limited has just commissioned the O.B. Lulu-Briggs ICT Centre for Digital Innovations inside Obonoma community for Akuku-Toru Local Government Area for Rivers State. This state-of-t...

item.title: Telecom Operators Attribute Service Disruptions to Vandalism Amid Rising Data Usage in Nigeria
item.description: Telecom operators for Nigeria don yarn say di wahala wey dey affect telephone services and internet connections no be because dem dey slack, but na because of constant fibre cuts, vandalism, and other...

item.title: ASEAN Leaders Gather in Cebu for 48th Summit Focused on Energy and Regional Issues
item.description: Regional leaders from ASEAN member countries have started arriving in Cebu for the 48th ASEAN Summit and associated meetings, hosted by the Philippines. President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. touched down at ...

item.title: Experts warn Bill C-22 may create cybersecurity vulnerabilities in Canada
item.description: Canadian cybersecurity experts, including the ethical hacking company Packetlabs, are cautioning that the government's Bill C-22, also known as the lawful access bill, could introduce serious security...

item.title: Federal High Court Schedules Hearing for Nafiu-Bala Gombe vs David Mark Leadership Dispute
item.description: The Federal High Court for Abuja don fix Friday, May 8, as the day to hear the case wey Mr. Nafiu-Bala Gombe file against Senator David Mark and some oda people over the leadership wahala for African ...

item.title: Concerns over governance, accountability and political influence in Tower Hamlets under Lutfur Rahman
item.description: The article examines the political situation in Tower Hamlets, East London, focusing on the leadership of directly elected mayor Lutfur Rahman ahead of local elections. It portrays Rahman as a dominan...

item.title: UKZN Graduate Nokuthula Dlamini Celebrates Success Despite Financial Struggles
item.description: Nokuthula Dlamini, 24, a graduate from the University of KwaZulu-Natal, proudly received her bachelor of arts honours in sociology despite facing significant financial challenges. Raised by a single m...

item.title: Zanzibar Adjusts 2025/2026 Budget to Match New Ministries
item.description: Zanzibar Government imeamua kurekebisha bajeti ya mwaka wa fedha 2025/2026 baada ya kupanua idara na mashirika chini ya Serikali ya Nane. Waziri wa Fedha na Mipango, Juma Malik Akili, alisema marekebi...

item.title: Pakistan expresses optimism over imminent US-Iran agreement and ongoing diplomatic efforts
item.description: Pakistan’s Foreign Office has expressed cautious optimism that an agreement between the United States and Iran could be reached in the near future, describing expectations as “sooner rather than later...

item.title: VP Shettima Endorses Senator Wadada to Succeed Gov. Sule in Nasarawa
item.description: The Vice President of Nigeria, Senator Kashim Shettima, don endorse Senator Ahmed Aliyu Wadada as the APC candidate for the 2027 Nasarawa governorship election. Dis endorsement come during the opening...

item.title: Kremlin Confirms Increased Security for Putin During Victory Day Celebrations
item.description: The Kremlin announced enhanced security measures for President Vladimir Putin ahead of the May 9 Victory Day celebrations in Russia, citing potential threats from Ukraine. Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Pes...

item.title: Farrer Byelection and Budget Challenges Highlight Political and Economic Uncertainty
item.description: As Australia approaches the 2026 budget, political and economic tensions are building. The government faces multiple pressures, including a Middle East conflict, rising fuel costs, interest rate incre...

item.title: Severe Flooding Forces Over 2,000 Residents into Emergency Shelters in Gqeberha
item.description: Severe storms have battered the Eastern Cape, particularly Gqeberha, leaving thousands of residents displaced and forcing them into emergency shelters. Over 2,000 people are currently taking refuge in...
